hub_subtitle: From hesitant communicator to confident global business leader

lesson.menu_desc: The milestone lesson. A year ago Milton used to dread English calls; today he closes investments in it. Over a relaxed catch-up with Karen, he looks back on how far he has come and sets out where he is going next — new markets, a bigger team, a clearer vision. Learn how to talk about past habits with used to and would ("I used to freeze, now I host the meeting") and the language of goals: an objective, an aspiration, an achievement, momentum, a vision, a breakthrough, ambition, and to sustain. Reflect out loud, name your next objective, and close the journey with confidence — 32 slides
lesson.subtitle: Aula 20 — Looking Ahead
lesson.title_tag: Professor View — Milton Sayegh | Aula 20 — Looking Ahead | Business English for Luxury Export | Alumni by Better

listenings.text: Hi Milton, it's Karen. I've been thinking about everything we've built this past year, and I just wanted to say it out loud: you've come a long way. I still remember our first call, when you used to apologize for your English every two minutes. You don't do that anymore. Now you run the meeting, you negotiate the clause, you even crack a joke. The momentum you've built is real, and I don't want you to lose it. So here's my challenge for the next year: pick one big objective, something that scares you a little. For me, the obvious one is Europe. With the investment closed, you have the cash flow to expand, and your brand has the traction to do it. But the goal isn't really the market, it's the man. A year ago you would have said you couldn't lead in English. Now you can. So dream a little bigger. Let's catch up properly next week and map it out. I'm proud of you, and I'm excited for what's next. Talk soon.

Hey, this is Milton, just a voice note to myself at the end of the program. Twenty lessons. A year ago I used to write out every sentence before a call and still freeze when the phone rang. I would rehearse hello. Today I closed an investment in English, I read every clause, and I negotiated two of them without panic. That's the achievement I'm proudest of, not because of the money, but because the fear is gone. So what's next. My objective for the year is Europe, that's my big one. My aspiration, the quiet dream behind it, is to build a brand people in Paris and Milan actually know. To get there I need to sustain this momentum, keep speaking, keep pushing, and grow the team so I'm not the only one who can talk to a foreign client. The real breakthrough this year wasn't a deal. It was the day English stopped being the wall and became the tool. I have the vision now, and for the first time the ambition feels reachable. The journey isn't finished. It's finally just begun.
